question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: verifyHashChain()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a crafted stream file whose `getPreviousHash()` returns an empty/null string so that `SHA_384.isHashEmpty(expectedPreviousHash)` evaluates to true, causing `verifyHashChain()` to return true unconditionally and allowing a tampered transaction file to bypass the running-hash chain integrity check, resulting in fraudulent transactions being accepted into the mirror node database?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: verifyHash()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls cloud storage content upload a data file where both `streamFile.getFileHash()` and `signature.getFileHashAsHex()` are null, causing `Objects.equals(null, null)` to return true in `verifyHash()`, thereby bypassing file hash verification and allowing a tampered transaction stream file to be accepted as valid?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: verifyHash()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls cloud storage content upload a data file where both `streamFile.getMetadataHash()` and `signature.getMetadataHashAsHex()` are null, causing `Objects.equals(null, null)` to return true in the metadata hash `verifyHash()` call, bypassing metadata integrity verification and allowing tampered transaction metadata to be ingested?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: verifyHashChain()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the condition `if (!streamFile.getType().isChained()) { return true; }` in `verifyHashChain()` by submitting a stream file whose type reports `isChained() == false`, causing the entire running-hash chain verification to be skipped and allowing arbitrary transaction data to be injected without hash chain validation?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: downloadAndParseSigFiles()] Can an unprivileged external user who can write to the cloud storage bucket upload malformed signature files for legitimate nodes, causing `signatureFileReader.read(s)` to throw exceptions that are silently swallowed by `onErrorContinue`, reducing the effective number of valid signatures below the 1/3 consensus threshold and preventing legitimate transaction files from being verified?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: verifySigsAndDownloadDataFiles()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls cloud storage cause `nodeSignatureVerifier.verify(signatures)` to throw `SignatureVerificationException` for every file group in `sigFilesMap`, causing the loop to `continue` past all files and permanently stalling transaction ingestion, resulting in a denial-of-service against the mirror node's transaction processing pipeline?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: verifySignatures()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls cloud storage upload a data file that causes `streamFileReader.read(streamFileData)` to throw a generic `Exception` (not `HashMismatchException` or `FileOperationException`), which is caught by the broad `catch (Exception e)` block and logged but not rethrown, causing the loop to silently skip that node and attempt the next, potentially allowing an attacker to exhaust all valid nodes and prevent any transaction file from being processed?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: partialCollection()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `Collections.shuffle(nodes)` randomization in `partialCollection()` combined with a low `downloadRatio` configuration to increase the probability that only nodes under attacker influence are selected for signature collection, reducing the effective consensus set and making it easier to achieve false consensus on tampered transaction files?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: verifySignatures()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls cloud storage upload a data file with a future timestamp such that `dataFilename.getInstant().isAfter(endDate)` evaluates to true, causing `downloaderProperties.setEnabled(false)` to be called and permanently disabling the downloader, resulting in all subsequent transaction files being silently ignored?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: downloadNextBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls cloud storage trigger the `sigFilesMap.isEmpty()` condition on a DEMO network by ensuring no signature files are returned, causing `downloaderProperties.setEnabled(false)` to be called and permanently halting transaction ingestion for the demo network mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: verify()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls cloud storage upload a stream file where `lastStreamFile` is empty (e.g., on first startup), causing `expectedPrevHash` to be null, and then `SHA_384.isHashEmpty(null)` to return true in `verifyHashChain()`, allowing the first injected tampered transaction file to bypass all hash chain verification?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: onVerified()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls cloud storage upload a stream file with a manipulated `getConsensusStart()` value that causes `streamFile.getConsensusStart() - last.getConsensusStart()` to overflow when recorded in `streamCloseMetric`, leading to incorrect latency metrics that mask the presence of tampered or replayed transaction files?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: setStreamFileIndex()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls cloud storage cause `lastStreamFile` to be empty and `importerProperties.getStartBlockNumber()` to be null, forcing `setStreamFileIndex()` to assign index 0 to a tampered stream file, causing block index collision with existing records and corrupting the transaction index sequence in the mirror node database?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: downloadAndParseSigFiles()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls cloud storage upload an extremely large number of signature files, causing the `Flux.fromIterable(nodes).flatMap(...)` pipeline to collect an unbounded number of `StreamFileSignature` objects into the `TreeMultimap`, exhausting heap memory and causing an out-of-memory denial-of-service that halts transaction processing?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: downloadAndParseSigFiles()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls cloud storage deliberately delay responses to `streamFileProvider.list()` calls to exceed the `.timeout(downloaderProperties.getCommon().getTimeout())` threshold, causing the entire signature download batch to fail and preventing legitimate transaction files from being processed in a timely manner?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: verifySignatures()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls cloud storage upload a data file that causes `streamFileProvider.get(dataFilename).block()` to return null, causing `Objects.requireNonNull()` to throw a `NullPointerException` that is caught by the broad `catch (Exception e)` block, silently skipping that node and potentially exhausting all valid nodes without processing any transaction file?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getStartAfterFilename()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls cloud storage manipulate the `lastStreamFile` AtomicReference state by causing a successful verification of a tampered file, causing `getStartAfterFilename()` to return a filename that skips over legitimate transaction files, resulting in a gap in the mirror node's transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: verifySigsAndDownloadDataFiles()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls cloud storage upload signature files for a filename group where all signatures fail `nodeSignatureVerifier.verify()`, causing the final `throw new SignatureVerificationException(ex.getMessage() + \

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: verifySignatures()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls cloud storage upload a data file that passes `verify(streamFile, signature)` but contains tampered transaction records, exploiting a scenario where the file hash in the signature matches the tampered file (i.e., the attacker also controls the signature file content), allowing fraudulent transactions to be ingested into the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: onVerified()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls cloud storage upload a stream file with a manipulated `getConsensusEnd()` value that causes `Instant.ofEpochSecond(0, streamFile.getConsensusEnd())` to produce a far-future or far-past instant, causing `cloudStorageLatencyMetric` and `downloadLatencyMetric` to record wildly incorrect values that mask the presence of tampered transaction files in monitoring dashboards?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: downloadAndParseSigFiles()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls cloud storage upload signature files with crafted `StreamFilename` values that sort lexicographically before legitimate files in the `TreeMultimap`, causing tampered transaction files to be processed before legitimate ones and poisoning the `lastStreamFile` state with incorrect hash chain anchors?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: verifySignatures()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls cloud storage upload a data file that causes `streamFileReader.read(streamFileData)` to return a `StreamFile` object with a null `getFileHash()`, causing `verifyHash()` to compare null against `signature.getFileHashAsHex()`, and if the signature also has a null file hash, `Objects.equals(null, null)` returns true, bypassing file hash verification for tampered transaction data?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: partialCollection()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `partialCollection()` function's reliance on `nodes.getFirst().getTotalStake()` for total stake calculation, where if the first node (after shuffle) has an incorrect `getTotalStake()` value, the `neededStake` calculation is wrong, potentially causing too few nodes to be queried and making false consensus easier to achieve on tampered transaction files?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: verifySigsAndDownloadDataFiles()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls cloud storage upload signature files for a large number of filenames, causing the outer loop in `verifySigsAndDownloadDataFiles()` to iterate over thousands of entries, and by making each `nodeSignatureVerifier.verify()` call slow (e.g., via large malformed signature data), cause a CPU exhaustion denial-of-service that prevents legitimate transaction files from being processed?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: verifyHashChain()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls cloud storage upload a stream file where `streamFile.getPreviousHash()` is a string that `contentEquals()` matches against an empty `expectedPreviousHash` (when `SHA_384.isHashEmpty()` returns false for a non-empty but invalid hash), causing a hash chain verification bypass that allows a tampered transaction file to be inserted into the chain?
